codex.name: Codex (OpenAI)
codex.notes: GPT-5.5 released 2026-04-23 — better mid-loop error recovery, 400K ctx. GPT-5.4 retained as cheaper fallback. gpt-5.4-mini used for embarrassingly-parallel fan-out (per-file anti-slop).

claude.name: Claude (Anthropic)
claude.notes: Opus 4.7 released 2026-04-16 — new tokenizer (~1.35x tokens), task budgets, xhigh effort tier. Sonnet 4.6 = workhorse. Haiku 4.5 = fan-out worker (200K ctx, near-frontier per dollar).

codex_plugin_cc.name: Codex Plugin for Claude Code (OpenAI official)
codex_plugin_cc.notes: OpenAI's official codex-plugin-cc — opt-in alternative to codex-mcp-server. Provides /codex:review and /codex:adversarial-review primitives. Set enabled=true and matching mcp_server entry to use.

anti_slop.note: Per-file scoring is embarrassingly parallel — fan out one worker per file.

devils_advocate.note: Frontier reasoning matters here — keep on the top-tier model.

gap_analysis.note: 10-dimension comparison doesn't need frontier — Sonnet/GPT-5.4 sufficient.

routing.note: tier maps to provider.{tier}_model: 'frontier'->model, 'fallback'->fallback_model, 'worker'->worker_model. Adding a new provider: add to providers{} and reference in routing/gates.

scoring.note: max_rounds reduced 3->2 in v3.0.0: GPT-5.5 mid-task self-correction means round 3 rarely changes verdicts.

concurrency.note: max_parallel_claude raised 2->3 to absorb Opus 4.7 tokenizer overhead (~1.35x). max_fanout_workers caps Haiku/gpt-5.4-mini parallelism for per-file gates.

codex.name: codex
codex.description: Wraps Codex CLI as MCP server for cross-model calls

claude_code.name: claude-code-mcp
claude_code.description: Wraps Claude Code as MCP server for cross-model calls

codex-plugin-cc.name: codex-plugin-cc
codex-plugin-cc.description: Official OpenAI Codex plugin for Claude Code (alternative to codex-mcp-server)

github.note: Used by `pipeline.js publish` to post gate results as GitHub commit statuses

bypass.note: SKIP_PIPELINE_CHECK=1 alone no longer bypasses — set PIPELINE_BYPASS_REASON='<text>' or use `pipeline.js bypass --reason '<text>'`. All bypasses are logged.
